title: From Hidden Superpower to Active Advantage: How to Connect, Transform and Action Your Data with Data 360
author: Ed Cho, Luis Perez Camacho

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: Turn your activatedData 360into business results. This guide shows you how to connect scattered data, transform it into customer insights, and automate actions that make you look like a mind reader. You’veactivatedData 360 in yourSalesforce Foundations. Congratulations! But if you’re like most admins, you’re probably looking at your Data 360 setup and wondering: “Okay, it’s on… now what? How do I actually make this help my business, serve customers better, and step into the data strategist role I’ve been dreaming of?” Don’t worry, we’ve got you. The path from activation to real business impact follows three phases:Connect → Transform → Action. By the end, your Data 360 won’t just be another tool gathering digital dust. It’ll be your secret weapon for delivering trusted intelligence that will make your CEO ask, “How did you know that?” Your Superpower Journey: Connect → Transform → Action The path from Data 360 activation to business impact follows three progressive phases, each building on the previous one: Connect:Unify all of your customer data and have them talking to each other. Expected outcome: “Finally, all of our scattered data is now in one place.” Transform:Turn messy, duplicate data into crystal-clear customer insights. That “John Smith” from your CRM and “J. Smith” from your payment system becomes one complete customer profile. Expected outcome: “We have a unified view of who our customers are now.” Action:Make your data work automatically to prevent problems and create opportunities Expected outcome: “Data 360 is delivering real business value.”

sections.heading: Phase 1: Connect – Bring Your Data Together
sections.text: The first step in your Data 360 journey is all about building a single source of truth for your customer, which is a unified 360-degree profile that combines everything your business knows about them. Once it’s in place, every workflow, report, automation, and AI-powered insight becomes faster, more accurate, and easier to scale. The beauty about Data 360 is that it can help you access three main sources of data to create this unified data foundation: 1. Your Salesforce CRM (Start Here – It’s Easy):Start with the data you know best — Accounts, Contacts, Leads, and Cases. Data 360 provides standard bundles for Sales Cloud and Service Cloud that intelligently map this data, automating much of the manual work and setting up your unified foundation. 2. Your Company’s Data Lake/Warehouse (This Is Where It Gets Exciting):This is where Data 360’s power truly shines. That valuable product usage, transaction, or logistics data that often lives inSnowflake,Google BigQuery,Amazon Redshift, orDatabricks?Data 360’szero-copycapabilities lets you virtually access that data without you having to duplicate it or build complex ETL processes. You get to leverage the investment your company has already made in its data infrastructure and enrich your customer profiles with even deeper insights. 3. Other Business Systems (On-Prem, 3rd Party Systems):Need data that lives in legacy, On-Premise systems, or in custom apps? Data 360 provideshundreds of prebuilt connectors, which gives you more flexible ways to unify all your critical customer data. Once you build this connected data foundation where all of your fragmented data lives it powers everything: For Sales:It can enrich Account pages with product usage data, identify cross-sell opportunities.For Service:It can power proactive case creation, provide agents with a complete customer history, and personalize support interactions.For Marketing:It can be used to build hyper-targeted audience segments for personalized email journeys, ad campaigns, and more.For Analytics:It can bring trusted, up-to-the-moment customer data to life by layering on visual, explorable, and actionable analytics and insights.For AI:The data foundation grounds AI Agents with trusted business context that helps them deliver relevant customer experiences.For You: You become the data strategist who always has the answer because you have all the data. The Bottom line: Connecting all of your data sources is the foundational step that makes every future project easier and more powerful.

sections.heading: Phase 2: Transform Your Data Into Unified Intelligence
sections.text: “Transforming your data” can sound complex and a little scary, but think of it as organizing your superhero utility belt. You’re taking all that connected data and adding value to it which makes it more powerful and useful for business and customers to understand and act on. There are three main ways to transform your data from “messy information” to “powerful business intelligence”: Make Sense of Your Data Sources (Customer 360 Data Model) Organizing different data sources is crucial but if it’s not easy to use, it won’t get used. Think of it like turning gold ore into beautiful jewelry that people actually want. You might have “contact” tables scattered everywhere: CRM Contacts and Leads, Marketing Cloud Subscribers, external system contacts. Instead of writing complex code to connect them, Data 360 lets you simply map each source to a proven template. The template automatically sets up relationships between tables, so when you need to take action on that data, everything just works—no difficult technical steps required. What this means for you: Your messy data becomes organized, usable intelligence without any coding. Solve the “Which John Smith?” Problem (Identity Resolution) Every business has this problem: “John Smith” in your CRM, “J. Smith” in billing, “johnsmith@company.com” in marketing, and “John S.” in support. Are these four different people or one customer you’re confusing and annoying? Data 360’s identity resolution is like having a detective that figures out these are all the same person and creates one complete customer profile. Before data transformation: Four confused customer records, missed opportunities, annoying duplicate emails. After data transformation: One complete customer story that helps you serve them “better”. Create Defined Business Metrics (Calculated Insights) Stop recalculating “Customer Lifetime Value” or “Health Score” every time someone asks for it. Set it up once in Data 360, and it updates automatically. What this means for you: Instead of spending hours on spreadsheets, you become the admin who has answers ready before people ask the questions. The Multiplier Effect of Transformed and Modeled Data Once your customer data is transformed and modeled, everything else becomes exponentially more powerful and much quicker to build and put into use: For Sales:Complete account intelligence showing product usage patterns, support history, and expansion signals in real-time.For Service: Service agents can see the complete customer journey before answering calls enabling personalized resolutions on first contact instead of generic troubleshooting.For Marketing:Precise audience segments based on actual behavior across all touchpoints.For AI: Clean, complete data that makes Agentforce agents deliver trusted experiences. This unified foundation doesn’t just improve current processes, It enables entirely new capabilities that weren’t possible with fragmented data.

sections.heading: Phase 3: Action-Put Your Data to Work
sections.text: With your unified data foundation in place, it’s time to deliver immediate value. We provide two powerful, real-world use cases below that you can build yourself. Data Activation Example #1: Enhance Your Account Page with Data from Your WarehouseThe Use Case:Your data lives in Snowflake and our sales rep is looking at an account page but has no idea if this customer is thriving or struggling with your product.The Solution:Using the zero-copy connection you established, add aDynamic Related Listto your Account Lightning page that displays“Product Utilization”data directly from Snowflake in real time. Your sales team gets critical engagement insights right where they work, without ever leaving Salesforce.The Result:Your sales team looks like mind readers who always know the right thing to say when serving their customers.Data Activation Example #2: Prevent Customer Churn Before it HappensThe Challenge:Customers often stop using your product for weeks before they actually cancel. How can you prevent customer churn before it’s too late?The Solution:Create aCalculated Insightin Data 360 to track the average product utilization score for each customer (using the data from your warehouse). Then build aData 360-Triggered Flow. If a customer’s utilization score drops below a certain threshold, the flow automatically creates a high-priority task for the account owner to follow up. This is proactive, data-driven automation at its best.The Result:You become the admin who prevented customers from cancelling because your early warning system caught problems before customers even complained. The Use Case:Your data lives in Snowflake and our sales rep is looking at an account page but has no idea if this customer is thriving or struggling with your product.The Solution:Using the zero-copy connection you established, add aDynamic Related Listto your Account Lightning page that displays“Product Utilization”data directly from Snowflake in real time. Your sales team gets critical engagement insights right where they work, without ever leaving Salesforce.The Result:Your sales team looks like mind readers who always know the right thing to say when serving their customers. The Challenge:Customers often stop using your product for weeks before they actually cancel. How can you prevent customer churn before it’s too late?The Solution:Create aCalculated Insightin Data 360 to track the average product utilization score for each customer (using the data from your warehouse). Then build aData 360-Triggered Flow. If a customer’s utilization score drops below a certain threshold, the flow automatically creates a high-priority task for the account owner to follow up. This is proactive, data-driven automation at its best.The Result:You become the admin who prevented customers from cancelling because your early warning system caught problems before customers even complained. Your connected, transformed data foundation can also power: Personalized marketing campaigns that actually workAI agents that give helpful answers because they know your businessProactive customer communications that prevent problemsReports that update themselvesInsights that help leadership make smarter decisions Read ourData 360 Self-Service Evaluation guidefor a more in depth walk through on how you can Connect, Transform and Action your data in Data 360. Want to explore Data 360 before implementing it in your Salesforce Foundations? Test all features safely in afree Data 360 Developer editionfirst. Remember: You’re not just managing data anymore, you’re building the data activation layer that will power your organization’s growth. That’s not system administration; that’s data strategy superhero work.

raw_html:
Turn your activated Data 360 into business results. This guide shows you how to connect scattered data, transform it into customer insights, and automate actions that make you look like a mind reader.

You’ve activated Data 360 in your Salesforce Foundations. Congratulations! But if you’re like most admins, you’re probably looking at your Data 360 setup and wondering: “Okay, it’s on… now what? How do I actually make this help my business, serve customers better, and step into the data strategist role I’ve been dreaming of?”

Don’t worry, we’ve got you. The path from activation to real business impact follows three phases: Connect → Transform → Action. By the end, your Data 360 won’t just be another tool gathering digital dust. It’ll be your secret weapon for delivering trusted intelligence that will make your CEO ask, “How did you know that?”

Your Superpower Journey: Connect → Transform → Action 

The path from Data 360 activation to business impact follows three progressive phases, each building on the previous one:

Connect: Unify all of your customer data and have them talking to each other. 

Expected outcome: “Finally, all of our scattered data is now in one place.”

Transform: Turn messy, duplicate data into crystal-clear customer insights. That “John Smith” from your CRM and “J. Smith” from your payment system becomes one complete customer profile. 

Expected outcome: “We have a unified view of who our customers are now.”

Action: Make your data work automatically to prevent problems and create opportunities

Expected outcome: “Data 360 is delivering real business value.”

Phase 1: Connect – Bring Your Data Together

The first step in your Data 360 journey is all about building a single source of truth for your customer, which is a unified 360-degree profile that combines everything your business knows about them. Once it’s in place, every workflow, report, automation, and AI-powered insight becomes faster, more accurate, and easier to scale.

The beauty about Data 360 is that it can help you access three main sources of data to create this unified data foundation: 

1. Your Salesforce CRM (Start Here – It’s Easy): Start with the data you know best — Accounts, Contacts, Leads, and Cases. Data 360 provides standard bundles for Sales Cloud and Service Cloud that intelligently map this data, automating much of the manual work and setting up your unified foundation.

2. Your Company’s Data Lake/Warehouse (This Is Where It Gets Exciting): This is where Data 360’s power truly shines. That valuable product usage, transaction, or logistics data that often lives in Snowflake, Google BigQuery, Amazon Redshift, or Databricks? Data 360’s zero-copy capabilities lets you virtually access that data without you having to duplicate it or build complex ETL processes. You get to leverage the investment your company has already made in its data infrastructure and enrich your customer profiles with even deeper insights.

3. Other Business Systems (On-Prem, 3rd Party Systems): Need data that lives in legacy, On-Premise systems, or in custom apps? Data 360 provides hundreds of prebuilt connectors, which gives you more flexible ways to unify all your critical customer data.

Why This Data Foundation Becomes Your Reusable Superpower

Once you build this connected data foundation where all of your fragmented data lives it powers everything: 

  • For Sales: It can enrich Account pages with product usage data, identify cross-sell opportunities.
  • For Service: It can power proactive case creation, provide agents with a complete customer history, and personalize support interactions.
  • For Marketing: It can be used to build hyper-targeted audience segments for personalized email journeys, ad campaigns, and more.
  • For Analytics: It can bring trusted, up-to-the-moment customer data to life by layering on visual, explorable, and actionable analytics and insights. 
  • For AI: The data foundation grounds AI Agents with trusted business context that helps them deliver relevant customer experiences. 
  • For You: You become the data strategist who always has the answer because you have all the data.

The Bottom line: Connecting all of your data sources is the foundational step that makes every future project easier and more powerful.

Phase 2: Transform Your Data Into Unified Intelligence

“Transforming your data” can sound complex and a little scary, but think of it as organizing your superhero utility belt. You’re taking all that connected data and adding value to it which makes it more powerful and useful for business and customers to understand and act on.  

There are three main ways to transform your data from “messy information” to “powerful business intelligence”:

  1. Make Sense of Your Data Sources (Customer 360 Data Model)

Organizing different data sources is crucial but if it’s not easy to use, it won’t get used. Think of it like turning gold ore into beautiful jewelry that people actually want.

You might have “contact” tables scattered everywhere: CRM Contacts and Leads, Marketing Cloud Subscribers, external system contacts. Instead of writing complex code to connect them, Data 360 lets you simply map each source to a proven template. The template automatically sets up relationships between tables, so when you need to take action on that data, everything just works—no difficult technical steps required.

What this means for you: Your messy data becomes organized, usable intelligence without any coding.

  1. Solve the “Which John Smith?” Problem (Identity Resolution)

Every business has this problem: “John Smith” in your CRM, “J. Smith” in billing, “johnsmith@company.com” in marketing, and “John S.” in support. Are these four different people or one customer you’re confusing and annoying?

Data 360’s identity resolution is like having a detective that figures out these are all the same person and creates one complete customer profile.

Before data transformation: Four confused customer records, missed opportunities, annoying duplicate emails.

After data transformation: One complete customer story that helps you serve them “better”.

  1. Create Defined Business Metrics (Calculated Insights)

Stop recalculating “Customer Lifetime Value” or “Health Score” every time someone asks for it. Set it up once in Data 360, and it updates automatically.

What this means for you: Instead of spending hours on spreadsheets, you become the admin who has answers ready before people ask the questions.

The Multiplier Effect of Transformed and Modeled Data

Once your customer data is transformed and modeled, everything else becomes exponentially more powerful and much quicker to build and put into use:

  • For Sales: Complete account intelligence showing product usage patterns, support history, and expansion signals in real-time.  
  • For Service: Service agents can see the complete customer journey before answering calls enabling personalized resolutions on first contact instead of generic troubleshooting. 
  • For Marketing: Precise audience segments based on actual behavior across all touchpoints. 
  • For AI: Clean, complete data that makes Agentforce agents deliver trusted experiences.

This unified foundation doesn’t just improve current processes, It enables entirely new capabilities that weren’t possible with fragmented data.

Phase 3: Action-Put Your Data to Work

With your unified data foundation in place, it’s time to deliver immediate value. We provide two powerful, real-world use cases below that you can build yourself.

  • Data Activation Example #1: Enhance Your Account Page with Data from Your Warehouse
    • The Use Case: Your data lives in Snowflake and our sales rep is looking at an account page but has no idea if this customer is thriving or struggling with your product.
    • The Solution: Using the zero-copy connection you established, add a Dynamic Related List to your Account Lightning page that displays “Product Utilization” data directly from Snowflake in real time. Your sales team gets critical engagement insights right where they work, without ever leaving Salesforce.
    • The Result: Your sales team looks like mind readers who always know the right thing to say when serving their customers. 
  • Data Activation Example #2: Prevent Customer Churn Before it Happens
    • The Challenge:  Customers often stop using your product for weeks before they actually cancel. How can you prevent customer churn before it’s too late? 
    • The Solution: Create a Calculated Insight in Data 360 to track the average product utilization score for each customer (using the data from your warehouse). Then build a Data 360-Triggered Flow. If a customer’s utilization score drops below a certain threshold, the flow automatically creates a high-priority task for the account owner to follow up. This is proactive, data-driven automation at its best.
    • The Result: You become the admin who prevented customers from cancelling because your early warning system caught problems before customers even complained.

How You Can Apply Your Superpower to Other Examples: 

Your connected, transformed data foundation can also power:

  • Personalized marketing campaigns that actually work
  • AI agents that give helpful answers because they know your business
  • Proactive customer communications that prevent problems
  • Reports that update themselves
  • Insights that help leadership make smarter decisions

Read our Data 360 Self-Service Evaluation guide for a more in depth walk through on how you can Connect, Transform and Action your data in Data 360. 

Want to explore Data 360 before implementing it in your Salesforce Foundations? Test all features safely in a free Data 360 Developer edition first. 

Remember: You’re not just managing data anymore, you’re building the data activation layer that will power your organization’s growth. That’s not system administration; that’s data strategy superhero work.
